executiveSummary: This SaaS Subscription Agreement grants Acme Corp. a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use CloudBase Inc.'s platform for a 12-month initial term with automatic annual renewal. The agreement presents High overall risk driven by a low liability cap (1× monthly fees), broadly drafted unilateral modification rights, and a 60-day termination-for-convenience notice window that works against the customer. Key financial terms are front-loaded with all fees non-refundable after payment. Legal counsel review is recommended before execution.
paymentTerms: $2,400/year billed annually in advance. All fees are non-refundable. Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month.
renewalTerms: Automatically renews for successive 12-month terms unless either party provides 90 days' written notice before the renewal date. CloudBase may increase fees on renewal with 60 days' notice.
terminationTerms: Either party may terminate for cause with 30 days' written notice to cure. Customer may terminate for convenience with 60 days' notice but is not entitled to a refund for the remaining term. CloudBase may suspend access immediately for payment default.
liabilityIssues: Aggregate liability is capped at the fees paid in the 1 month immediately preceding the claim — significantly below potential business exposure.
indemnificationIssues: Customer broadly indemnifies CloudBase for claims arising from Customer's data and use of the platform. CloudBase indemnifies for IP infringement only, with carve-outs for modifications by Customer.
confidentialityTerms: Mutual confidentiality obligations survive termination for 3 years. Exceptions for publicly available information and regulatory disclosures.
governingLaw: State of Delaware, USA. Disputes resolved by binding arbitration under AAA rules; class action waiver included.
missingClauses: Data portability and deletion on termination
unusualClauses: Provider may modify platform features or pricing unilaterally with 30 days' notice
Class action waiver in arbitration clause limits collective remedies
No SLA credit mechanism — outages have no financial consequence for Provider

topRisks.title: Minimal Liability Cap
topRisks.explanation: Provider's aggregate liability is capped at 1× monthly subscription fees regardless of the nature or extent of the loss. For an annual $2,400 contract this means the cap is $200 — far below any realistic business damage.
topRisks.whyItMatters: If CloudBase loses your data or causes a material business interruption, your maximum recovery is $200 even if your actual loss is far larger.
topRisks.suggestedNextStep: Negotiate a higher cap — typically 12× monthly fees minimum — and add carve-outs for willful misconduct, gross negligence, and data breach.
topRisks.supportingQuote: Provider's aggregate liability shall not exceed the fees paid by Customer in the one (1) month immediately preceding the claim.

topRisks.title: Unilateral Modification Rights
topRisks.explanation: CloudBase may modify platform features, pricing, or terms with only 30 days' notice. Customer's only recourse is to terminate — and forfeits any prepaid fees.
topRisks.whyItMatters: Material changes to the product or price can be imposed mid-term. If Customer disagrees, the only exit costs the remaining annual subscription.
topRisks.suggestedNextStep: Request that modifications to pricing or material terms require mutual written agreement, not unilateral notice.
topRisks.supportingQuote: CloudBase reserves the right to modify the Service or these Terms upon thirty (30) days' written notice. Continued use after the notice period constitutes acceptance.

topRisks.title: No Service Level Guarantee
topRisks.explanation: The agreement contains no uptime commitments, no SLA credits, and no remedies for service unavailability. CloudBase's liability for downtime is zero by contract.
topRisks.whyItMatters: A SaaS platform that is down costs the Customer in productivity but the agreement provides no contractual recourse.
topRisks.suggestedNextStep: Request a minimum 99.9% uptime SLA with credit schedule (e.g., 10% credit per 1 hour of excess downtime).
topRisks.supportingQuote: THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED 'AS IS' WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED.

topRisks.title: Auto-Renewal with 90-Day Notice Window
topRisks.explanation: The contract automatically renews annually. To cancel, Customer must give 90 days' prior written notice — meaning the cancellation deadline is 3 months before the renewal date.
topRisks.whyItMatters: Missing the 90-day window locks Customer into another full 12-month term and another $2,400 payment.
topRisks.suggestedNextStep: Calendar the cancellation deadline immediately. Consider negotiating a shorter window (30–45 days is typical).
topRisks.supportingQuote: This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year terms unless either party provides ninety (90) days' prior written notice of non-renewal.

topRisks.title: Broad Customer Data Indemnification
topRisks.explanation: Customer must indemnify CloudBase against any third-party claims arising from Customer's data or use of the platform. This is broadly worded and could expose Customer to significant liability.
topRisks.whyItMatters: If a third party sues CloudBase based on data Customer uploaded or Customer's use of the platform, Customer is contractually required to defend and hold harmless CloudBase.
topRisks.suggestedNextStep: Narrow the indemnification to claims arising from Customer's willful misconduct or gross negligence. Add a cap on Customer's indemnification exposure.
topRisks.supportingQuote: Customer shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless CloudBase from any claims, damages, or expenses arising from Customer's use of the Service or Customer's data.

actionItems: Negotiate liability cap to minimum 12× monthly fees with gross negligence and data breach carve-outs
Request SLA addendum with 99.9% uptime guarantee and credit schedule
Calendar auto-renewal cancellation deadline (90 days before 2025-01-15 = 2024-10-15)
Request a mutual written amendment requirement for any pricing or feature changes
Add a GDPR / data processing addendum before handling any EU personal data
Request data portability and certified deletion provisions for contract termination
Have legal counsel review the arbitration and class action waiver clauses

citations.quote: Provider's aggregate liability shall not exceed the fees paid by Customer in the one (1) month immediately preceding the claim.
citations.relevance: This 1× monthly cap is the most significant risk in the agreement — far below any realistic business exposure.

citations.quote: CloudBase reserves the right to modify the Service or these Terms upon thirty (30) days' written notice.
citations.relevance: Unilateral modification rights allow material changes to price and features with minimal notice.

citations.quote: This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year terms unless either party provides ninety (90) days' prior written notice of non-renewal.
citations.relevance: A 90-day window is unusually long; most SaaS agreements use 30–60 days.

citations.quote: THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED 'AS IS' WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED.
citations.relevance: Combined with no SLA, this means zero contractual recourse for service outages.

disclaimer: Synth is not legal advice or financial advice. It is a document review aid. Consult a qualified professional before making decisions.
